global_invariants: electronic kick, snare or clap, closed hi-hat, bass, and pads
no tempo, meter, or key changes
no risers, impacts, hard mutes, dropouts, patch swaps, or mastering changes at section boundaries

60.form_timing: A from 0:00 to 0:20, B from 0:20 to 0:40, and A-prime from 0:40 to 1:00

90.form_timing: A from 0:00 to 0:30, B from 0:30 to 1:00, and A-prime from 1:00 to 1:30

pairs.template: Create an exactly {DURATION}-second instrumental electronic / beat-driven pop track at 120 BPM in 4/4; use a stable four-chord loop, a clearly identifiable monophonic synth-lead hook, electronic kick, snare or clap, closed hi-hat, bass, and pads; follow {FORM_TIMING}; A establishes the hook and groove, B keeps the same harmony, tempo, meter, instrumentation, sound palette, arrangement density, dynamics, and mix, and A-prime returns to a lightly varied version of A in A's original register; keep the lead hook's pitch-class sequence, rhythm, note durations, phrase count, and synthesizer patch unchanged across sections; {TARGET_CLAUSE}; make the section transitions clean, with no risers, impacts, hard mutes, dropouts, key changes, patch swaps, or mastering changes; no vocals.

clauses.control: During the B section only, play the synth-lead hook in the same middle register used in A
clauses.intervention: During the B section only, play the synth-lead hook exactly one octave above the register used in A

b_core_invariants: lead pitch-class sequence and phrase count

pairs.template: Create an exactly {DURATION}-second instrumental electronic / beat-driven pop track at 120 BPM in 4/4; use a stable four-chord loop, a clearly identifiable monophonic synth-lead hook, electronic kick, snare or clap, closed hi-hat, bass, and pads; follow {FORM_TIMING}; A establishes the hook and groove with steady eighth-note closed hi-hats, B keeps the same harmony, tempo, meter, instrumentation, sound palette, lead hook, bass pattern, kick pattern, snare pattern, dynamics, and mix, and A-prime returns to a lightly varied version of A with steady eighth-note closed hi-hats; keep the closed-hi-hat sound, accent pattern, stereo position, and perceived section-level hat loudness unchanged; {TARGET_CLAUSE}; make the section transitions clean, with no drum fills, open-hi-hat substitutions, risers, impacts, hard mutes, dropouts, tempo changes, key changes, patch swaps, or mastering changes; no vocals.

clauses.control: During the B section only, the closed hi-hat plays steady eighth notes
clauses.intervention: During the B section only, the closed hi-hat plays steady sixteenth notes

b_core_invariants: closed-hi-hat sample, accent topology, and panning
